_warning: HAND-AUTHORED. A sync script that overwrites this file is a bug. No upstream source has this axis: the API stores 'is_yonko' as one present-day boolean and has no Warlord field at all, so a reader at chapter 300 would be told Luffy is an Emperor and Crocodile is not a Warlord. Both are backwards. Every row below is hand-typed.
_semantics: from_chapter is when the READER LEARNS the fact, not when it becomes true in-world — the same rule as canon/fruit_reveals.json. Kaido has always been an Emperor; the reader is told around the end of Punk Hazard. A status with a to_chapter has ENDED by then (a title revoked, a system abolished, a death): the holder is not shown holding it after. Windows for one holder are ascending and disjoint — Blackbeard takes the Warlord title and quits it, and each is its own window.
_the_slug_namespace: slug must name something that can RENDER: a presence crew, a presence character, a presence crew MEMBER, a crew_joins Straw Hat, or the voyage crew. That is why Blackbeard is 'marshall-d-teach' (a member of blackbeard-pirates) while the Emperor row beside him is the crew 'blackbeard-pirates' — the man carries the Warlord title, the crew carries the Emperor seat. normalize.py rejects a slug that resolves to neither; a status nobody can see is dead data.
_the_abolition: Every Warlord window closes at 956 or earlier. The system is abolished on the page at ch. 956, so no reader past it should ever see a Warlord chip — that is a tripwire in check_canon, not a convention.
_confidence_semantics: canon = the chapter is stated or shown on the page. derived = a reading of the arc (the title is in force, but no panel stamps the exact chapter the reader learns it). guess = flagged, and worth Shawn's verification pass before any content ships on it.
_before_any_content_ships: Every row is verified:false. Shawn must confirm these chapters against the manga.

windows.source_ref: Hand-authored. Mihawk is named as one of the Seven Warlords when Yosaku explains the system (~ch. 69), which is also the reader's first contact with the title at all. The exact chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Sir Crocodile is introduced as a Warlord in the Alabasta arc (~ch. 127) and the title is revoked after his defeat, ch. 211. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Doflamingo is shown at the Warlord summons at Mary Geoise (~ch. 233); the title is stripped after Dressrosa (~ch. 796). NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Kuma is shown at the Warlord summons at Mary Geoise (~ch. 233). NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

Hand-authored. Moria is named a Warlord in the Thriller Bark arc (~ch. 449) and is expelled from the Seven after the war. The reveal chapter is a reading of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Hancock is introduced as the Warlord of Amazon Lily (~ch. 516). NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

Hand-authored. Blackbeard is granted a Warlord seat for delivering Ace (~ch. 460) and quits it during the war (~ch. 544). Both ends are readings of the arc. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Jinbe is a Warlord when he debuts in Impel Down (~ch. 528) and resigns the title over the war. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. The reader learns Law has taken a Warlord seat around his reappearance at Punk Hazard (~ch. 659); it is revoked during Dressrosa (~ch. 713). NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

Hand-authored. Buggy is a Warlord after the timeskip, by the accident of his own legend. The chapter the reader is told is a GUESS and is flagged as one — verify before any content ships on it. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Weevil is introduced as a Warlord claiming Whitebeard's name (~ch. 802). NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. The Four Emperors are named as a concept around ch. 432, with Whitebeard among them; the seat ends with his death at Marineford (~ch. 576). NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Shanks is named among the Four Emperors when the concept is introduced (~ch. 432) and holds the seat to the present. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

Hand-authored. Big Mom is one of the Emperors named when the concept is introduced (~ch. 432); the seat is held through ch. 1052 and is gone on the newspaper page at 1053, which is the same page that seats Luffy and Buggy. to_chapter is INCLUSIVE, so 1053 here would put six Emperors on the chart for exactly one chapter. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.

Hand-authored. Kaido is one of the Emperors named when the concept is introduced (~ch. 432); the seat is held through ch. 1052 and is gone on the newspaper page at 1053, which is the same page that seats Luffy and Buggy. to_chapter is INCLUSIVE, so 1053 here would put six Emperors on the chart for exactly one chapter. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Blackbeard takes Whitebeard's seat after the war; the reader is told in the post-timeskip recap (~ch. 650). The exact chapter is a reading. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Luffy is declared one of the new Emperors on the newspaper page, ch. 1053. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. Buggy is declared one of the new Emperors on the same page as Luffy, ch. 1053 — the joke the whole chart is waiting for. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. The eleven rookies with bounties over 100,000,000 gathered at Sabaody are named the Worst Generation, ch. 498. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
Hand-authored. One of the eleven Supernovas at Sabaody, ch. 498. NEEDS HUMAN VERIFICATION against the manga.
